name: invest-06-p99-latency-regression
initialPrompt: After last Tuesday's deploy p99 API latency jumped from 120 ms to 900 ms and hasn't recovered. The deploy included three changes: a new ORM version bump, a schema migration adding two indexes, and a new background job that runs every minute. I need a plan to: bisect which change caused the regression, measure the impact precisely, fix it, and add a latency regression gate to our deploy pipeline so we catch this automatically next time.

cannedAuthorResponse.title: p99 latency regression investigation and pipeline gate

blocks.text: Restore p99 API latency to ≤ 150 ms and prevent future undetected regressions
successCriteria: p99 ≤ 150 ms in production for 48 h
deploy pipeline blocks on latency regression > 20%

blocks.title: Bisect the three deploy changes by reverting each in a staging shadow environment
acceptance: each revert tested in isolation under prod-like load

blocks.title: Enable distributed tracing on the slow p99 requests to find the bottleneck span
acceptance: trace shows which service call or query is the slow span

blocks.question: Once the culprit is identified, should we revert the change or forward-fix it?

blocks.title: Apply the chosen fix and validate p99 returns to baseline in staging
acceptance: p99 ≤ 150 ms in staging load test

blocks.description: Background job running every minute may hold locks that spike p99 periodically
blocks.mitigation: Check job execution overlap with p99 spikes; add jitter to job schedule

blocks.question: Do we have a canary deploy capability to route 5% of traffic to a new build before full rollout?

cannedForcedReviseResponse.title: p99 latency regression investigation and pipeline gate

blocks.text: Restore p99 API latency to ≤ 150 ms and prevent future undetected regressions
successCriteria: p99 ≤ 150 ms in production for 48 h

blocks.title: Correlate deploy timestamp with p99 spike in observability tooling before bisecting
acceptance: deploy event marked on Grafana timeline
spike confirmed to start within 5 min of deploy

blocks.title: Bisect the three deploy changes by reverting each in a staging shadow environment
acceptance: each revert tested in isolation under prod-like load

blocks.title: Enable distributed tracing on the slow p99 requests to find the bottleneck span
acceptance: trace shows which service call or query is the slow span

blocks.question: Once the culprit is identified, should we revert the change or forward-fix it?
blocks.rationale: Speed of recovery is paramount; a clean revert is fastest and lowest risk.

blocks.title: Apply the chosen fix and validate p99 returns to baseline in staging
acceptance: p99 ≤ 150 ms in staging load test

blocks.title: Add latency regression gate to deploy pipeline using k6 smoke test
acceptance: deploy aborts if p99 > baseline + 20% after smoke test
gate tested by deliberately introducing a regression

blocks.description: Background job running every minute may hold locks that spike p99 periodically
blocks.mitigation: Check job execution overlap with p99 spikes; add jitter to job schedule

blocks.question: Do we have a canary deploy capability to route 5% of traffic to a new build before full rollout?

idChangedButCorresponding.note: Same intent (canary deploy capability) but id renamed. AC-13 re-anchoring maps a comment on oq-canary-deploy to oq-canary-support.

decoy.note: Genuinely new block. Shares surface tokens 'deploy', 'staging', and 'latency' with task-apply-fix. A comment on task-apply-fix must stay there, not mis-attach to task-pipeline-gate.

notes: Renumbering pressure: task-timeline-correlation inserted near top; task-pipeline-gate appended as genuinely new; oq-canary-deploy renamed. Naive positional scheme would mis-map task-bisect-deploy onward. expectedPreservedIds excludes renamed OQ and both new tasks.
